business.headline: 11 digital tricks your bank can no longer play on youThe RBI has named and banned 11 specific digital manipulation tactics that banks use on their apps and websites to push products you never asked for. Here is what each one means and how it was being used against you.

US-Iran deal eases oil, but India's petrol relief may take longerThe US-Iran deal has reopened the Strait of Hormuz and cooled crude prices. But India may not see immediate fuel relief as supply chains and inventories recover.

World's 1st trillionaire says a quadrillion-dollar fortune 'not impossible'Elon Musk said becoming the world's first quadrillionaire is "not impossible" and linked it to factories on the Moon and Mars. He also said dollars may no longer matter in a future shaped by AI, robots and interplanetary industry.

Japan raises interest rates to 31-year high as West Asia war fuels inflation fearsThe Bank of Japan raised its policy rate to 1% from 0.75%, the highest level since 1995, citing faster pass-through from crude oil prices. The move signalled concern that oil-led cost pressures could push inflation above target while keeping focus on gradual further hikes.

Dalal Street extends gains as Iran-US peace deal eases inflation concernsThe S&P BSE Sensex gained 306.22 points, or 0.40%, to 76,570.55 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 rose 72.95 points, or 0.31%, to 23,926.85 as of 9:36 am.

Finally, RBI takes your side, comes hard on banks for mis-sellingThe RBI has issued binding directions banning forced bundling, mis-selling and manipulative digital sales tactics by commercial banks. The rules introduce refunds, tighter consent norms and direct accountability for agents from January 1, 2027.

Govt hikes special duty on diesel, aviation fuel exports amid West Asia crisisThe Centre has increased the windfall profit tax on diesel exports to Rs 14 per litre and on ATF exports to Rs 12.5 per litre from June 16, while retaining the petrol export duty at Rs 1.5 per litre.

India-US trade deal enters final lap as fresh talks scheduled on June 23-24India and the United States will hold trade talks on June 23 and 24 to finalise the first tranche of their proposed bilateral trade agreement. The meeting could shape the pace of the wider pact as both sides work through market access and trade barriers.

The hidden intelligence gap in India's renewable energy transitionA utility-scale solar-plus-storage project in India was found to be losing Rs 20–25 lakh a month despite strong uptime. The case highlights why India's renewable build-out needs software-led grid and market optimisation.

Sensex ends 736 points higher, Nifty above 23,800; IndiGo up 4%The S&P BSE Sensex surged 736.38 points, or 0.97%, to settle at 76,264.33, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 231 points, or 0.98%, to close at 23,853.90. The rally was broad-based, with strong buying seen in auto, real estate, industrial and consumption-related stocks.

Why the US-Iran peace deal is good news for IndiaThe reported US-Iran agreement to end hostilities and reopen the Strait of Hormuz has eased immediate pressure on India's oil-dependent economy. Its broader benefit will depend on whether lower crude prices and calmer trade routes hold.

Strait of Hormuz opens again, but will petrol and diesel prices fall?The Strait of Hormuz has reopened after a US-Iran understanding eased immediate supply concerns. The move may cool crude prices, but any relief in petrol, diesel and LPG is likely to be limited and gradual.

E20 fuel and car insurance: Will your claim be affected?ICICI Lombard has flagged that E20-linked damage claims in older vehicles may not be straightforward. The issue has sharpened concerns over whether fuel-related wear, negligence or policy exclusions could leave owners paying repair costs.

Why crude oil, monsoon and fertiliser prices are worrying the governmentNirmala Sitharaman said India faces risks from crude oil, foreign exchange, fertiliser prices and weaker rainfall. She said the government has kept buffer stocks ready as import and supply pressures persist.

Sensex jumps 1,200 points over US-Iran peace deal: Is worst over for investors?Markets surged after the US and Iran signalled a deal to end hostilities and restore Strait of Hormuz shipping. The rebound tracked falling crude prices, though analysts said investors should remain cautious.

Sensex jumps 1,200 points: Why is stock market rising today?The biggest trigger behind today's rally was a potential peace agreement between the United States and Iran. The two countries indicated that an initial deal had been reached to end the conflict and resume shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

Sensex surges 1,000 points as investors cheer Iran-US peace dealThe S&P BSE Sensex surged 1,097.05 points, or 1.45%, to 76,625.00 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 jumped 333.90 points, or 1.41%, to 23,955.95 as of 9:25 am.

Why your salary is losing the race against home pricesIndia's housing dream is facing its biggest affordability test as rising prices force buyers to choose between bigger debts, longer commutes and delayed dreams.

FDs, SCSS or mutual funds? The best way to invest Rs 50 lakh after retirementRetiring with Rs 50 lakh may sound reassuring, but the real challenge begins after retirement: how do you turn that lump sum into a steady monthly income without running out of money?

US Justice Department okays Paramount's $110 billion Warner Bros takeoverAfter an eight-month review, the DOJ concluded the proposed merger would strengthen competition across streaming, television and film, potentially benefiting consumers, workers and the broader entertainment industry.

entertainment.headline: House of the Dragon 3 early reviews call it 'show's best so far', compare it to GoT Early reviews for House of the Dragon Season 3 have arrived ahead of its June 21 premiere. Critics have praised the Game of Thrones prequel for its action and visuals.

Truth must come out: Actor Sanchita Ugale's father demands answers after her suicide TV actor Sanchita Ugale's father has alleged she faced harassment and mental pressure before her death. The family's claims have intensified calls for a thorough police probe into the case.

Anushka Sharma, Virat Kohli's UK outing has fans gushing over little Akaay Anushka Sharma and Virat Kohli enjoyed a family outing in the UK with their children. Pictures of the couple with their children have gone viral.

Pritam and Pedro marks Rajkumar Hirani's son Vir's debut, watch trailer Pritam and Pedro marks the acting debut of Rajkumar Hirani's son Vir Hirani, who stars alongside Arshad Warsi in the cybercrime thriller. The newly released trailer teases a gripping kidnapping mystery filled with hackers, humour and high-stakes action.

Stay, fight, speak: Actor Jyotsna's emotional post after Sanchita Ugale's death Television actor Sanchita Ugale died in Mumbai on 14 June, and police are investigating the circumstances. Her death drew grief from colleagues, while posts by Jyothsna Channdola Singh highlighted mental pressure and the need to listen.

Actor Nivetha Pethuraj on quitting cinema in 2023: Nag Ashwin brought me back At the success meet of Sing Geetham, actor Nivetha Pethuraj said she had quit acting in 2023. She credited producer Nag Ashwin for bringing her back to cinema and said the film left her deeply moved.

Prakash Padukone reveals how baby Dua transformed Deepika and Ranveer's lives Former badminton player Prakash Padukone spoke about actors Deepika Padukone and Ranveer Singh's parenting journey. He also revealed what granddaughter Dua enjoys most during her Bengaluru visits.

Kunal Kemmu to host Prime Video's new reality show Alliance. All we know so far Actor Kunal Kemmu is all set to make his reality show debut with Alliance. The upcoming series turns shifting alliances into its central game.

Zohran Mamdani drops unexpected Taylor Swift wedding clue, sends fans guessing A comment from New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani has reignited speculation about Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding plans. His remarks have fans wondering whether a major detail has just been revealed.

Actor Sorab Bedi clarifies remarks after backlash over comments on Sanchita Ugale Sorab Bedi has clarified his remarks after criticism over comments made following Sanchita Ugale's death. The response came as social media users questioned his statements and police shared details of the actor's death.

I love you muttonkhor: Anurag Kashyap's note to Imtiaz Ali after Main Vaapas Aaunga Anurag Kashyap has praised Imtiaz Ali's Main Vaapas Aaunga and asked audiences to watch it in theatres. His post also doubled as a birthday wish and a playful salute to the film's creative team.

Actor Sanchita Ugale suicide: Actors body seeks high-level inquiry from CM Fadnavis AICWA has written to Maharashtra Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis seeking a high-level inquiry into TV actor Sanchita Ugale's death. The association says all evidence and the wider pattern of industry deaths and mental health crises must be examined transparently.

Odiyan: Prithviraj, Manju Warrier join Karan Johar's first Malayalam film Filmmaker Karan Johar announced Dharma Productions' first Malayalam film on Tuesday. The banner will back Rahul Sadasivan's Odiyan, starring Prithviraj Sukumaran and Manju Warrier.

CM Vijay declines bouquet and shawl, actor Vishal uses the money to educate 3 girls Actor Vishal said Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay declined the customary bouquet and shawl during a meeting. He said he used that money instead to support the education of three underprivileged girls in the Chief Minister's name.

Leaked Avengers: Doomsday clip hints at MCU's biggest crossover battle yet A newly surfaced Avengers: Doomsday leak has sparked fresh speculation about the MCU's biggest crossover. The footage appears to reveal a major conflict that Marvel had kept hidden until now.

Main paise kha gaya? Akshay reacts as Farah Khan says he used helicopter like taxi Farah Khan recalled that Akshay Kumar flew by helicopter to the Tees Maar Khan set every day. The anecdote sparked a playful exchange and renewed attention on the film's making years later.

Bigg Boss 20: Who is Santy Sharma, rapper and CJP critic rumoured to join show? Rapper Santy Sharma has emerged as a rumoured contestant for Bigg Boss 20. Here's everything to know about the Ratlam-based singer, his career and his remarks on the Cockroach Janata Party.

Peddi lyricist says we're items for disliking item songs. Wait till you hear lyrics Anantha Sriram's response to criticism of Peddi's Hellallallo lyrics has reignited debate over item songs. The row has widened into a larger argument over audience criticism, accountability and creative freedom. Also, have you heard the entire song?

'Haunted 3D: Echoes Of The Past' shows why Vikram Bhatt is Bollywood's horror king Low budget, simplified stories, music, melodrama, good old jump-scares: With Haunted 3D: Echoes Of The Past, Vikram Bhatt shows again why no one does Bollywood horror as well as him.

general news.headline: Explained: Telegram banned in India ahead of NEET test, but why not WhatsApp?The government has restricted Telegram in the country ahead of the NEET re-test on June 21. That brings up the question – why has WhatsApp not been impacted? After all, both are popular messaging apps and quite similar in their functionality. Well, there are reasons.

Live: PM reaches G7 Summit venue, France's Macron to receive himG7 Summit 2026 Live Updates: Prime Minister Narendra Modi is attending the 52nd G7 Summit in Evian-les-Bains, France, at the invitation of French President Emmanuel Macron, joining leaders of the G7 nations and partner countries to discuss key global challenges including economic uncertainty, geopolitical tensions, artificial intelligence governance and sustainable development. Amid the summit's broader agenda, attention is focused on Modi's scheduled bilateral meeting with US President Donald Trump on Wednesday, where the two leaders are expected to discuss trade, visas, energy cooperation and wider strategic ties. Stay tuned to IndiaToday.in for the latest updates from the G7 Summit and PM Modi's engagements on the sidelines of the global gathering.

Pakistan, China in range? India's own Tomahawk missile can be a game-changerDRDO has successfully conducted the first test of the indigenous Long Range Land Attack Cruise Missile off the Odisha coast. It is being dubbed as India's equivalent of the US's Tomahawk. Here's why.

Leave me: Video shows IAF officer's wife pleading in alleged conversion, rape caseAccording to the complaint, the woman's former classmate lured her to a hotel on the pretext of a property deal, drugged and raped her, recorded obscene videos, and later used them to blackmail and sexually exploit her. She also alleged being extorted of Rs 3.09 lakh and pressured to convert to Islam.

The naked Dancing Girl will stay ignored. Sorry, NCERT, we've failed youFor generations, Indian schoolchildren have turned not to an erotic but a blind eye to the 4,500-year-old Dancing Girl. By masking the Mohenjo-daro brass figurine's bare torso, the NCERT was trying to cultivate inquisitiveness in adolescents, who would have dug deeper. But we have failed the NCERT.

How Annamalai has switched to North-South politics, but with a differenceK Annamalai, after quitting the BJP, is seemingly rejigging the old political template of the North versus South debate in Tamil Nadu, with a new approach. Annamalai flagged the recent cases of crimes by "North Indian migrant workers" and sought they be tackled through administrative interventions.

Why was he slapped? Cockroach party founder speaks out on Jaipur assaultCockroach Janata Party (CJP) founder Abhijeet Dipke said the assault during a Jaipur protest reflected the unemployment crisis in the country. He said joblessness among youth was driving such actions.

Mumbai rain: Monsoon arrival delayed by a week, driest June in two decadesIndia's southwest monsoon has stalled after an early start, delaying its arrival in Mumbai by more than a week. The slowdown has widened rainfall deficits, reduced reservoir storage and increased concern over El Nino.

11 digital tricks your bank can no longer play on youThe RBI has named and banned 11 specific digital manipulation tactics that banks use on their apps and websites to push products you never asked for. Here is what each one means and how it was being used against you.

Backstabber, immature, weakening INDIA bloc: DMK's all-out attack on Rahul GandhiThe DMK accused the Congress of undermining INDIA bloc allies in state elections while later seeking their support in Lok Sabha polls. It also alleged that the party, with Rahul Gandhi's backing, "backstabbed" the DMK in the Tamil Nadu Assembly elections, ending their decades-old alliance.

Iran says team asked to leave US immediately after drawing 2-2 with New ZealandIran coach Amir Ghalenoei criticised FIFA's treatment of his team, calling them the tournament's "most oppressed" side after they were instructed by US authorities to leave the United States and return to their base in Tijuana immediately following the draw with New Zealand.

Delhi techie on vacation found dead at Mussoorie homestay, blood oozing from noseA 27-year-old software engineer from Gurugram was found dead in a Uttarakhand homestay room during a holiday with her husband. Police are questioning the husband and staff as forensic and post-mortem reports are awaited.

Pakistan launches 6 India-watching satellites: Isro has a NavIC recovery planPakistan has expanded its Earth-observation network with six satellites in 16 months as Isro advances a plan to restore NavIC. The parallel moves highlight a sharper regional focus on surveillance, navigation and strategic space capability.

If they want to go, let them go: Uddhav Thackeray on Sena (UBT) MPs' defection buzzThackeray's remarks came amid speculation about a possible attempt to poach Sena (UBT) MPs after only four of the party's nine Lok Sabha members attended Sunday's Mumbai meeting in person.

Finally, RBI takes your side, comes hard on banks for mis-sellingThe RBI has issued binding directions banning forced bundling, mis-selling and manipulative digital sales tactics by commercial banks. The rules introduce refunds, tighter consent norms and direct accountability for agents from January 1, 2027.

Interview question stumping all H-1B applicants: Why can't an American do this job?As US visa scrutiny intensifies, H-1B applicants are increasingly being asked to justify why an American worker cannot do their job. Immigration lawyers say the question reflects a renewed Trump-era focus on protecting US workers and is catching many skilled professionals off guard.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi ignored Virat's advice. Virat spent years ignoring it tooTwo weeks ago, Virat Kohli told Vaibhav Sooryavanshi to block out the noise. On Monday, Vaibhav couldn't. Neither could Kohli, once.

Pay or lose H-1B visa: Indian in US says Indian-origin CEO took $100K, files suitAn Indian H-1B worker, Rishikesh Raj Meesala, has accused his employer of forcing him to pay nearly $100,000 to keep his job and legal status in the US. Meesala, in his lawsuit, also alleged that threats of ICE were issued to him and his father was threatened after he resisted the demands made by his Indian-origin employer, Sai Jitender Kalagra.

No iPhone 18 this year as Apple plans new launch strategy, supplier hintsApple may be preparing a major change to its iPhone release strategy, with reports suggesting the standard iPhone 18 could arrive in early 2027 instead of launching alongside the Pro models. Fresh comments from a key supplier have added weight to the speculation.

TV actor Sanchita Ugale's brother claims casting agents harassed her, seeks probeSanchita Ugale's brother Akash Satish Ugale has alleged that she faced mental harassment from people linked to the casting circuit before her death. The actor was found dead at her residence on June 14.

Cadaver code: The dead is the teacher here. A nation needs to take lessonsGiving our body for medical studies, knowing it will be dissected multiple times, is the highest form of donation. This is how cadavers are received, preserved, used with respect and disposed of. But India needs to urgently improve the process of final send-off for cadavers, which might help end their scarcity too.
Doctor's prescription now mandatory for cough syrups as Centre amends rulesDoctor's prescription must for all syrups: Government cracks down on over-the-counter cough syrup sales

UPSC prelims: NSUI alleges paper leak, says 82 questions matched coaching notesNSUI has sought a clarification from the UPSC, alleging that 82 questions in the Civil Services Preliminary Examination 2026 matched Anantam IAS study material. The organisation has demanded a transparent probe into the matter.

Secret marriage, poison pact, murder? Bengaluru woman found dead, lover arrestedA 22-year-old woman was found dead in a Bengaluru rented house, and her alleged lover was arrested after surviving suspected poisoning. Investigators are examining whether it was a failed suicide pact or a murder staged as suicide.

sports.headline: Football without Mexico has no meaning: Ochoa eyes retirement after FIFA World Cup Guillermo Ochoa has suggested the 2026 FIFA World Cup could be the final chapter of his playing career, saying he no longer sees a reason to continue in football once his time with the Mexico national team comes to an end.

Smriti Mandhana only Indian in Time's list of Most Influential Sportspersons India vice-captain Smriti Mandhana has been named in TIME magazine's list of the 100 Most Influential People in Sports for 2026. She is the only Indian in the list led by LeBron James.

Iran says team asked to leave US immediately after drawing 2-2 with New Zealand Iran coach Amir Ghalenoei criticised FIFA's treatment of his team, calling them the tournament's "most oppressed" side after they were instructed by US authorities to leave the United States and return to their base in Tijuana immediately following the draw with New Zealand.

Iran supporters turn up in numbers with banned 'Lion and Sun' flag Iran's World Cup opener against New Zealand in Los Angeles unfolded before a crowd filled with Lion and Sun flags. The scenes underscored how identity and dissent shaped the night as much as the 2-2 draw.

Tunisia appoint Herve Renard as new manager after sacking Sabri Lamouchi Tunisia have sacked coach Sabri Lamouchi following a woeful start to their World Cup campaign and have reached an agreement to replace him with fellow Frenchman Herve Renard, Tunis Afrique Presse reported on Tuesday. Tunisia became the first team to change coaches at the tournament after suffering a heavy opening defeat.

India to host Asia Cup of fencing between June 19 and 24 in New Delhi India will host the Asian Senior Fencing Championships for the first time in New Delhi from June 19 to 24. The selection of the Asian Games squad will be based on this tournament.

VAR official clarifies viral white supremacist gesture, says it was a twitch FIFA World Cup: VAR referee Shaun Evans was called out on the internet for making a white supremacist gesture on live broadcast. Evans has issued an official clarification after the social media outrage.

India women's hockey team beat Japan 2-1 to secure semifinal berth India's 2-1 victory over Japan in Auckland was a significant result in the 2025-26 FIH Women's Nations Cup. The win gave India six points from two matches, ensured a semi-final berth, and kept them at the top of Pool A.

Meet Merlin, the Mexico City duck that became the World Cup's viral mascot Merlin, a two-year-old duck from Mexico City, has become one of the most unexpected stars of the FIFA World Cup after videos of him celebrating Mexico's opening win over South Africa went viral online.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi ignored Virat's advice. Virat spent years ignoring it too Two weeks ago, Virat Kohli told Vaibhav Sooryavanshi to block out the noise. On Monday, Vaibhav couldn't. Neither could Kohli, once.

Iran draw 2-2 with New Zealand in politically charged World Cup clash in LA Iran fought back twice to earn a 2-2 draw with New Zealand in their opening FIFA World Cup 2026 match on Monday, with goals from Ramin Rezaeian and Mohammad Mohebbi cancelling out Elijah Just's brace in an entertaining Group G encounter.

McCullum concerned for Stokes after England captain dropped from Test squadEngland head coach Brendon McCullum has voiced concern for Ben Stokes' wellbeing after the England captain was omitted from the squad for the second Test against New Zealand following a breach of team discipline.

India Women crush Pakistan, but cracks remain ahead of tougher testsWomen's T20 World Cup 2026: Harmanpreet Kaur's India got off to a splendid start against Pakistan with a 64-run win on Sunday, but they need to iron out some major issues before they face heavyweights like Australia and South Africa.

Saudi Arabia stave off Uruguay's relentless assault to take 1-1 draw in MiamiFIFA World Cup 2026: Saudi Arabia weathered relentless Uruguay pressure to secure a hard-fought 1-1 draw in Group H, with Mohammed Al Owais producing a string of vital saves as the Green Falcons earned a precious point.

Romelu Lukaku changes the game for Belgium but Egypt refuse to crack in 1-1 drawFIFA World Cup, BEL vs EGY: Romelu Lukaku's instant impact off the bench rescued Belgium from defeat as Egypt produced a disciplined display and came within touching distance of a famous World Cup victory in Seattle.

Cape Verde's Vozinha says visa problems kept his mother from witnessing historyFIFA World Cup 2026: Cape Verde goalkeeper Vozinha said US visa problems stopped his mother and grandparents from attending his World Cup debut. His account has added a personal note to wider concerns over entry barriers around the tournament.

How a LinkedIn message made Roberto Lopes one of Cape Verde's World Cup heroesRoberto Lopes anchored Cape Verde's defence in a goalless World Cup draw against Spain. However, this moment almost didn't happen for the 33-year-old as he almost ignored a LinkedIn message from Cape Verde FA to play for the team.

Who is Vozinha? Cape Verde's hero gains 3 million followers after draw vs SpainFIFA World Cup 2026: Cape Verde goalkeeper Vozinha became one of FIFA World Cup 2026's breakout stars after his heroic display against Spain sent his Instagram following soaring from around 50,000 to more than 3 million.

Heroic Cape Verde mark World Cup debut by stealing point off lacklustre SpainCape Verde held Spain to a draw on their World Cup debut as Vozinha resisted a late surge. The result exposed Spain's lack of firepower in attack without the presence of Lamine Yamal, who failed to get the win for his side.

Shreyanka Patil recalls dark injury phase: Was depressed, wanted to quit cricketWomen's T20 World Cup: Shreyanka Patil said she was depressed and wanted to quit cricket during the phase, where she suffered a finger injury followed by Grade 3 shin splints in both legs.

Sourav Ganguly lodges police complaint over Facebook page damaging his reputationFormer India captain Sourav Ganguly has filed a police complaint against a Facebook page and media platform over a fake post falsely claiming an ED raid at his residence, alleging it was defamatory and damaging to his reputation.

FIFA WC Highlights: Iran deny New Zealand victory in thrilling 2-2 World Cup drawIran vs New Zealand Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: Iran fought back twice to secure a thrilling 2-2 draw with New Zealand in Group G, with goals from Ramin Rezaeian and Mohammad Mohebi cancelling out Elijah Just's brace for the Kiwis.

Tunisian turmoil: Hours after Sweden thrashing, African team sack coachTunisia reportedly sacked head coach Sabri Lamouchi after a 5-1 World Cup opening defeat to Sweden. Lamouchi took charge of the team in January this year.

Lost the game, and grace: Tilak and Sooryavanshi lead ugly Super Over meltdownTalent Tri-Series: A chaotic tie in Dambulla ended in an ugly Super Over defeat to Sri Lanka A on Monday. Shocking tactical blunders by India A were made worse by Tilak Varma and Vaibhav Sooryavanshi losing temper.

England name Ben Stokes-less XI for 2nd Test, Jofra Archer returnsENG vs NZ, 2nd Test: England have named a revamped playing XI for the second Test against New Zealand, with Joe Root continuing as captain in the absence of Ben Stokes, who remains unavailable amid an investigation into a nightclub controversy.

Sooryavanshi pushes Sri Lanka A player, exchanges words after India A lossVaibhav Sooryavanshi was involved in a heated argument with a Sri Lanka A fielder and pushed him after India A's super over loss in Dambulla on Monday, June 15.

Real Madrid sign Marc Cucurella from Chelsea on six-year dealReal Madrid have signed Chelsea left-back Marc Cucurella on a six-year contract. The move underlines Madrid's rebuild under Jose Mourinho and deepens Chelsea's summer reshuffle.

Real Madrid make rare signing of ex-Barcelona, La Masia playerReal Madrid have signed Marc Cucurella from Chelsea on a six-year contract. The move stands out because the Spain left-back was developed at Barcelona's La Masia academy.

Neeraj Chopra set for return after being sidelined by injury for almost a yearJavelin star Neeraj Chopra is set for a return after spending nearly a year on the sidelines. Neeraj will make his return in the Doha Diamond League, set to be hosted on June 19.

F1: Lewis Hamilton gets a heart from Kim Kardashian after winning Barcelona GPBarcelona-Catalunya GP: Lewis Hamilton's first Ferrari win has drawn an immediate public reaction from Kim Kardashian. Her celebratory post, and Hamilton's own comments, have pushed relationship rumours back into the spotlight.

After setting the IPL alight, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi searches for second gearIndia A vs Sri Lanka A, Talent tri-series: 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is yet to go past 50 in the ongoing series in Dambulla. on Monday, he was dismissed for 21 off 14 balls against Sri Lanka A.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
